key_insight: The paper builds the first large spin-polarized electronic-structure database for stable halide double perovskites, revealing wide tunability in band gaps, magnetism, and bonding across chemistry space.
engineering_relevance: This dataset can accelerate screening of energy-material candidates for photovoltaics, photocatalysis, and radiation detection by linking composition to electronic and magnetic functionality.
method: Hybrid-functional DFT for >2,500 predicted-stable Cs2BB'X6 halide double perovskites
Computed spin-polarized density of states and classified band-gap/magnetic behavior
Used LOBSTER bonding analysis plus UMAP visualizations and an interactive app
evidence_signal: The abstract reports >2,500 compounds, including 719 with visible-range band gaps and 118 half-metals, providing a broad evaluation set for structure-property trends.
watch_item: The database is limited to compounds predicted stable by the τ tolerance factor and to Cs-based halide double perovskites, so coverage may not generalize to all perovskite chemistries.
paper_title: Spin-Polarized Electronic Structure and Chemical Bonding Data for 2,500+ Halide Double Perovskites
